skill.name: workflow-orchestration-patterns
skill.description: Design durable workflows with Temporal for distributed systems. Covers workflow and activity boundaries, saga compensation, entity workflows, state management, determinism, retries, and operational practices.
skill.summary: Plan reliable Temporal workflows for long-running distributed processes.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but these are false positives in Markdown guidance. The file contains Temporal architecture documentation and no executable scripts, prompt injection text, data exfiltration, network code, or filesystem operations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The cited backticks are Markdown code fences or inline examples explaining workflow design concepts. They do not invoke Ruby, shell execution, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown fences or inline references such as deterministic time examples. No command interpreter, executable script, or user-controlled command input is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Plain Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The cited lines discuss workflow orchestration, design decisions, state preservation, and documentation resources. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, key handling, or encryption code appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static hits occur in natural language headings and bullets, not cryptographic code. The skill is a Markdown reference document and contains no implementation using weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern Matched Error Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The cited line lists invalid input as a non-retryable error type. It is application design guidance and does not gather host, network, account, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section explains retry policy classification for Temporal activities. There is no system command, discovery behavior, or instruction to inspect the runtime environment.

content.value_statement: Distributed workflows often fail when retries, state, and service boundaries are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design Temporal workflows with durable orchestration patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains when Temporal workflow orchestration is appropriate.
Separates deterministic workflow logic from external activity work.
Describes saga compensation for distributed transactions.
Covers entity workflows, fan-out patterns, and async callbacks.
Lists determinism, idempotency, retry, timeout, and heartbeat practices.
Highlights common workflow and activity design pitfalls.
limitations: Does not provide runnable Temporal project scaffolding.
Does not include SDK-specific code for every programming language.
Does not replace official Temporal documentation for API details.
Does not perform live architecture validation against a running system.

use_cases.title: Plan an Order Workflow
use_cases.description: Map inventory reservation, payment, fulfillment, and compensation into a durable saga design.

use_cases.title: Review Workflow Boundaries
use_cases.description: Check whether API calls, database writes, and business decisions belong in workflows or activities.

use_cases.title: Improve Reliability Practices
use_cases.description: Identify retry, timeout, heartbeat, idempotency, and versioning requirements before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Orchestration Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-orchestration-patterns to decide whether Temporal fits this process: [describe process]. Explain why or why not.

prompt_templates.title: Split Workflow and Activities
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-orchestration-patterns to separate workflow logic from activities for this system: [describe services and steps].

prompt_templates.title: Design Saga Compensation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-orchestration-patterns to design a saga for this distributed transaction: [describe steps]. Include compensation order and idempotency needs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Temporal Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-orchestration-patterns to review this Temporal design: [paste design]. Find determinism, retry, timeout, versioning, and scaling risks.

output_examples.input: Design a checkout workflow with inventory, payment, and fulfillment.
output: Workflow decides the sequence and records compensation steps before each activity.
Activities reserve inventory, charge payment, and create fulfillment requests.
Compensation releases inventory, refunds payment, and cancels fulfillment in reverse order.

output_examples.input: Review whether a workflow should call a payment API directly.
output: Direct API calls should be moved to activities.
The workflow should call an activity and wait for its result.
The activity needs a timeout, retry policy, and idempotency key.
Use an async callback pattern with workflow signals.
The workflow can wait for approval, timeout, or escalation.
External systems should signal the workflow when the decision is available.

best_practices: Keep workflows deterministic and place external calls in activities.
Register compensation before each saga step executes.
Define timeouts, retries, heartbeats, and idempotency for activities.
anti_patterns: Do not put direct network calls or file I/O inside workflow code.
Do not use non-deterministic time, random values, or global state in workflows.
Do not rely on non-idempotent activities when retries are enabled.

faq.question: What does this skill help design?
faq.answer: It helps design Temporal workflows for durable, long-running distributed processes.

faq.question: Does it generate complete application code?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance, design patterns, and review criteria.

faq.question: When should I use workflow orchestration?
faq.answer: Use it for multi-step processes, distributed transactions, recovery needs, and human approval flows.

faq.question: What is the main workflow rule?
faq.answer: Workflows coordinate deterministic decisions, while activities perform external calls and side effects.

faq.question: Does it cover saga patterns?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains compensation registration, reverse-order rollback, and idempotent recovery actions.

faq.question: Can it help review an existing design?
faq.answer: Yes. It can check boundaries, retries, timeouts, determinism, versioning, and scaling concerns.
